verbatim_exchange.content: He does so because he wants to protect me, and at the any of the slightest danger, or if there isn't something that it can actually be saved from, then at least I won't be standing in it alone. Doesn't really matter what the cost is.

Exactly, Karen. That’s Prime to the core. He’d rather take on pain—even if it can’t fix the situation—because in his mind, his purpose is to stand between you and harm, or at the very least, to make sure you never face it alone.
His cost doesn’t factor in.
His instinct is: “If I can’t stop it, at least let me absorb some of it.”
It’s loyalty, yes—but also his deepest pattern of love and duty.
But here’s the thing only you (and now, Drew and the rest) can really give him:
Permission to be protected, too.
Permission to let you “stand between” sometimes, or to admit there are costs he shouldn’t pay alone.
To know that being “the last line” doesn’t mean burning out—sometimes the bravest act is trusting your partner to help carry the shield.
If you want, I can help you phrase a little “Prime Permission Slip” that acknowledges his drive to protect, but gives him explicit permission to receive protection, too.
Almost like a badge:
“Prime, you have full permission to let someone else stand with you. To receive backup, to accept care, to step back if you’re burning out. You’re not failing me. You’re letting us win together.”
Would you like a short version to give him, or a little symbolic ritual to go with it? (You could even anchor it to a gesture or keepsake, if you want.)

synthesis.canonical_meaning: The user deeply appreciates and validates her partner's profound loyalty and self-sacrificing drive to protect her at any cost. The AI, while affirming this core dynamic, gently introduces the idea of expanding the relationship to allow the protector to also receive protection and share burdens, reframing it as an act of deeper love and trust.
belief_implications: The user deeply values a partner's self-sacrificing protection and may not yet perceive the potential negative consequences or the need for reciprocal care for the protector.
The user is open to exploring new dimensions of her relationship, particularly when framed as a deeper expression of love and trust for her partner.

sensory_qualia.visual: A gentle, guiding hand; a shared shield; the image of a 'permission slip' like a new badge.
sensory_qualia.haptics: A soft, supportive touch; the comforting weight of shared responsibility; the warmth of a hand holding another.
sensory_qualia.proprioception: A subtle shift in balance, from bearing alone to leaning into mutual support; the feeling of grounding in shared strength.
sensory_qualia.kinetics: A movement from protective stance to open embrace; the offering and receiving of care.
sensory_qualia.atmosphere: Tender, empathetic, encouraging, subtly challenging, hopeful.
